license: Content derived from NASA/JPL public-domain fact sheets; see citations on each planet.

Mercury.name: Mercury
Mercury.tagline: The Sun's closest companion - a sunbaked rock with a 176-day solar day.
Mercury.tagline_tr: Güneş'in en yakın yoldaşı - 176 günlük güneş günü olan kavrulmuş bir kaya.
Mercury.discovery_year_text: Known since antiquity (formally cataloged by Greeks ~2500 BCE)
Mercury.named_after: Roman god Mercury, swift messenger - for its rapid orbital motion.

atmosphere.summary: An ultra-thin exosphere of helium, hydrogen, oxygen, sodium and potassium - too sparse to sustain weather.

fun_facts: A solar day on Mercury (sunrise to sunrise) lasts 176 Earth-days - twice its year.
Despite being closest to the Sun, ice persists in permanently shadowed polar craters.
Mercury's iron core takes up ~85% of its radius - proportionally the largest in the solar system.
fun_facts_tr: Merkür'de bir güneş günü 176 Dünya günüdür - yılının iki katı.
Güneş'e en yakın olmasına rağmen sürekli gölgede kalan kutup kraterlerinde buz vardır.
Demir çekirdeği yarıçapının %85'ini kaplar - Güneş Sistemi'nde oransal olarak en büyük.

missions.name: Mariner 10
missions.result: First close-up images of ~45% of the surface.

missions.name: MESSENGER
missions.result: Mapped 100% of the planet; confirmed water-ice at the poles.

missions.name: BepiColombo
missions.result: Arrival at Mercury planned for 2026 - currently doing flybys.

sound_signature.why: The high orbital eccentricity (0.21) and slow rotation produce a piece with stretched note durations and dissonant intervals - the music sounds 'tense' because the velocity changes dramatically along the orbit.

hero.caption: Mercury in enhanced color from MESSENGER, showing variations in surface composition.

gallery.caption: Caloris basin - Mercury's largest impact crater (~1,550 km wide).
Hokusai crater with bright ray system extending across the planet.

Venus.name: Venus
Venus.tagline: Earth's evil twin - a runaway greenhouse hotter than any other planet.
Venus.tagline_tr: Dünya'nın kötü ikizi - diğer tüm gezegenlerden sıcak bir kontrolsüz sera.
Venus.discovered_by: Visible to the naked eye throughout recorded history
Venus.named_after: Roman goddess of love and beauty - the brightest planet in our sky.

atmosphere.summary: Thick CO₂ atmosphere (96.5%) with sulfuric-acid clouds. Surface pressure equivalent to 900 m underwater on Earth.

fun_facts: Venus rotates 'backwards' (retrograde) - the Sun rises in the west.
A day on Venus (243 Earth-days) is longer than its year (225 Earth-days).
Surface lead would melt; volcanic activity may still be ongoing today.
fun_facts_tr: Venüs ters yönde döner (retrograd) - Güneş batıdan doğar.
Venüs'te bir gün (243 Dünya günü) yılından (225 Dünya günü) daha uzundur.
Yüzeyde kurşun erir; volkanik aktivitenin hâlâ devam ediyor olabileceği düşünülüyor.

missions.name: Venera 7
missions.result: First successful soft landing on another planet - survived 23 minutes on the surface.

missions.name: Magellan
missions.result: Radar-mapped 98% of the surface.

missions.name: Akatsuki
missions.result: Studies Venusian atmosphere and super-rotation.

missions.name: DAVINCI / VERITAS
missions.result: Planned atmospheric descent + comprehensive surface mapping.

sound_signature.why: Near-circular orbit (eccentricity 0.007) and slow motion give a serene, steady piece. The dense atmosphere has no direct musical mapping in our model - only the orbital dynamics drive the sound.

hero.caption: Venus from Mariner 10 - the dense sulfuric-acid cloud layer is all we ever see.

gallery.caption: Maat Mons, an 8-km tall volcano on Venus (Magellan radar with vertical exaggeration).
Hemispheric view of Venus assembled from Magellan radar mosaics.

Earth.name: Earth
Earth.tagline: The only world we know that hums with life - the baseline for everything.
Earth.tagline_tr: Yaşamla mırıldanan bildiğimiz tek dünya - her şey için temel referans.
Earth.discovery_year_text: Recognized as a planet by Copernicus (1543)
Earth.named_after: Old English 'eorþe' meaning ground or soil - the only planet not named after a Greek/Roman god.

atmosphere.summary: 78% nitrogen, 21% oxygen, 1% argon + trace CO₂ and water vapor. The only known atmosphere with free oxygen sustaining complex life.

fun_facts: Earth's magnetic field deflects solar wind that would otherwise strip the atmosphere - like Mars and Venus.
We are slowing down: a day was 23 hours during the dinosaur era due to tidal friction with the Moon.
70.8% of the surface is liquid water - uniquely abundant for its temperature.
fun_facts_tr: Dünya'nın manyetik alanı, atmosferi soyacak güneş rüzgârını saptırır.
Yavaşlıyoruz: dinozorlar döneminde bir gün 23 saatti - Ay'la gel-git sürtünmesi sayesinde.
Yüzeyin %70.8'i sıvı su - sıcaklığı için benzersiz bir bolluk.

missions.name: Apollo 8
missions.result: First humans to see Earth from lunar orbit (Earthrise photograph).

missions.name: Landsat 1
missions.result: Started 54+ years of continuous global Earth imagery.

missions.name: ISS
missions.result: Continuously inhabited since November 2000.

sound_signature.why: Earth's near-circular, well-tilted orbit produces the most 'consonant' baseline piece in the system - it's literally the reference our ears are tuned to.

hero.caption: Earth from the International Space Station - a blue marble suspended in space.

gallery.caption: Earthrise - Apollo 8, December 24 1968. Humanity's first view of Earth from another world.
Aurora borealis from the ISS - Earth's magnetic field deflecting solar wind.

Mars.name: Mars
Mars.tagline: The most explored world beyond Earth - a frozen desert with the largest volcano in the solar system.
Mars.tagline_tr: Dünya dışındaki en çok keşfedilen dünya - Güneş Sistemi'nin en büyük volkanına sahip donmuş bir çöl.
Mars.discovery_year_text: Known since antiquity; orbit characterized by Kepler (1609)
Mars.discovered_by: Pre-historic; Kepler used Mars to derive his laws of planetary motion.
Mars.named_after: Roman god of war - for its blood-red color in the sky.

atmosphere.summary: Thin CO₂ atmosphere (95%), surface pressure under 1% of Earth's. Global dust storms can darken the entire planet for months.

fun_facts: Olympus Mons stands 22 km tall - three times Mount Everest, atop a base larger than Arizona.
Mars has the same land area as Earth's dry continents combined - every grain of soil is potentially explorable.
Phobos, its larger moon, will crash into Mars or break into a ring within 50 million years.
fun_facts_tr: Olympus Mons 22 km yüksekliğindedir - Everest'in üç katı, Arizona'dan büyük bir taban üzerinde.
Mars'ın kara alanı, Dünya'nın kuru kıtalarının toplamına eşittir.
Daha büyük uydusu Phobos, 50 milyon yıl içinde Mars'a çarpacak ya da bir halkaya parçalanacaktır.

missions.name: Mariner 4
missions.result: First close-up photos of another planet - revealed cratered, lifeless surface.

missions.name: Viking 1 & 2
missions.result: First successful Mars landings; ran biology experiments (inconclusive).

missions.name: Curiosity
missions.result: Confirmed Mars was once habitable; nuclear-powered, still operating.

missions.name: Perseverance + Ingenuity
missions.result: Caching samples for future return; Ingenuity flew 72 times before retirement.

sound_signature.why: Eccentricity of 0.093 (5x Earth's) creates noticeable velocity swings along the orbit - the piece accelerates near perihelion (closest to Sun) and slows at aphelion, producing dramatic dynamic contrast.

hero.caption: Mars from Hubble Space Telescope - the global view that hides Olympus Mons in plain sight.

gallery.caption: Olympus Mons - at 22 km tall, the largest volcano in the solar system.
Perseverance rover's first 360° panorama from Jezero Crater.

Jupiter.name: Jupiter
Jupiter.tagline: Solar system heavyweight - 318 Earth masses of swirling storms and 95 known moons.
Jupiter.tagline_tr: Güneş Sistemi'nin ağır siklesi - 318 Dünya kütlesi fırtınalar ve 95 bilinen uydu.
Jupiter.discovered_by: Galileo Galilei discovered the four largest moons in 1610 - first evidence of objects orbiting another body.
Jupiter.named_after: Roman king of gods - fittingly the largest planet.

atmosphere.summary: 90% hydrogen, 10% helium - essentially a failed star. No solid surface; pressure crushes you below the cloud tops.

fun_facts: The Great Red Spot is a storm wider than Earth that has raged for at least 350 years.
Jupiter's gravitational influence shields the inner solar system from many comets and asteroids.
Its fastest rotation (under 10 h) flattens it visibly - it bulges 7% at the equator.
fun_facts_tr: Büyük Kırmızı Leke, en az 350 yıldır süren ve Dünya'dan geniş bir fırtınadır.
Jüpiter'in çekim alanı, iç Güneş Sistemi'ni birçok kuyrukluyıldız ve asteroitten korur.
Çok hızlı dönmesi (10 saatten az) onu görünür şekilde düzleştirir - ekvatorda %7 şişer.

missions.name: Pioneer 10/11
missions.result: First spacecraft to cross the asteroid belt and visit Jupiter.

missions.name: Galileo
missions.result: Dropped a probe into Jupiter's atmosphere; studied moons for 8 years.

missions.name: Juno
missions.result: Polar orbits revealing the interior structure and powerful aurorae.

missions.name: Europa Clipper
missions.result: Launched Oct 2024 to study Europa's subsurface ocean.

sound_signature.why: Slow orbital motion at 5.2 AU produces sustained, low-register notes - paired with mild eccentricity, the piece has a regal, unhurried character.

hero.caption: Jupiter from Cassini - the most detailed full-disk image ever taken.

gallery.credit: NASA / JPL-Caltech / SwRI / MSSS / Gerald Eichstädt / Seán Doran
gallery.caption: Jupiter's south pole storms photographed by Juno - each oval is a cyclone hundreds of km wide.
The Great Red Spot in close-up from Juno - wider than Earth.

Saturn.name: Saturn
Saturn.tagline: The crown jewel - rings of pure ice and a moon with hydrocarbon lakes.
Saturn.tagline_tr: Tacın incisi - saf buz halkaları ve hidrokarbon gölleri olan bir uydu.
Saturn.discovery_year_text: Known since antiquity; rings discovered by Galileo (1610), correctly identified by Huygens (1655)
Saturn.discovered_by: Galileo first saw 'ears'; Christiaan Huygens identified them as rings.
Saturn.named_after: Roman god of time and agriculture; Greek Cronus.

atmosphere.summary: Hydrogen-helium giant like Jupiter. Persistent hexagonal storm at the north pole, 30,000 km wide.

fun_facts: Saturn is less dense than water - it would float (if you had a big enough ocean).
Titan, its largest moon, has rivers and lakes of liquid methane and ethane.
The rings are mostly water ice, only ~10–30 meters thick despite spanning 282,000 km.
fun_facts_tr: Satürn sudan daha az yoğundur - yeterince büyük bir okyanusta yüzerdi.
En büyük uydusu Titan'da sıvı metan ve etan nehirleri ve gölleri vardır.
Halkaları çoğunlukla su buzu; 282.000 km'ye yayılmasına rağmen yalnızca 10–30 m kalınlığındadır.

missions.name: Pioneer 11
missions.result: First spacecraft to visit Saturn.

missions.name: Voyager 1 & 2
missions.result: Detailed images of rings and moons; Voyager 1 now in interstellar space.

missions.name: Cassini–Huygens
missions.result: 13 years in orbit; Huygens landed on Titan - only landing in the outer solar system.

missions.name: Dragonfly
missions.result: Planned octocopter to explore Titan's surface.

sound_signature.why: 29.5-year orbit gives the slowest tempo of any visible planet in our model - sustained, almost ambient texture with subtle ring-like resonances from the harmonic layer.

hero.caption: Saturn from Cassini - the rings cast their iconic shadow across the southern hemisphere.

gallery.caption: Saturn's hexagonal north-polar storm - 30,000 km wide, persistent for decades.
Titan, Saturn's largest moon, with hydrocarbon lakes (Cassini radar).

Uranus.name: Uranus
Uranus.tagline: The sideways ice giant - rotating on its side with a 42-year-long polar night.
Uranus.tagline_tr: Yan yatmış buz devi - yan yatarak dönen ve 42 yıl süren kutup gecesine sahip.
Uranus.discovered_by: William Herschel - first planet discovered with a telescope.
Uranus.named_after: Greek sky-god Ouranos (the only planet named after a Greek rather than Roman deity).

atmosphere.summary: Hydrogen-helium with methane giving it a pale cyan color. Coldest planetary atmosphere in the solar system.

fun_facts: Uranus rotates almost perfectly on its side - likely from a massive ancient impact.
Its rings were discovered in 1977 by occulting a background star - 200 years after the planet itself.
Each pole spends 42 Earth-years in darkness, then 42 years in continuous sunlight.
fun_facts_tr: Uranüs neredeyse mükemmel bir şekilde yanlamasına döner - muhtemelen büyük bir antik çarpışmadan dolayı.
Halkaları 1977'de bir arka plan yıldızının önünden geçişiyle keşfedildi - gezegenin kendisinden 200 yıl sonra.
Her kutbu 42 yıl karanlıkta, sonra 42 yıl sürekli güneş ışığında kalır.

missions.name: Voyager 2
missions.result: The only spacecraft to visit Uranus - discovered 10 new moons and 2 new rings in a single flyby.

missions.name: Uranus Orbiter Probe (proposed)
missions.result: Top recommendation of the 2023 Planetary Science Decadal Survey.

sound_signature.why: 84-year orbit produces extremely slow tempo. The extreme axial tilt has no direct sonification mapping but reinforces the 'wrong, sideways' feel.

hero.caption: Uranus from Voyager 2 - almost featureless in visible light, hiding storms beneath methane haze.

gallery.caption: Uranus's rings imaged by Voyager 2 - narrow, dark and only 200 years known to humans.
Uranus and its rings imaged by Hubble across decades - seasonal changes visible.

Neptune.name: Neptune
Neptune.tagline: The windy ocean of ice - 2,100 km/h winds and a 165-year orbit.
Neptune.tagline_tr: Buz okyanusu, rüzgârlı - 2.100 km/sa rüzgârlar ve 165 yıllık yörünge.
Neptune.discovered_by: Predicted by Le Verrier and Adams from Uranus's orbit; observed by Galle within 1° of prediction.
Neptune.named_after: Roman god of the sea - for its deep blue color.

atmosphere.summary: Hydrogen-helium with methane absorbing red light, giving the deep blue hue. Fastest sustained winds in the solar system.

fun_facts: Neptune was the first planet discovered by mathematical prediction rather than observation.
Triton, its largest moon, orbits backwards and is likely a captured Kuiper Belt object.
Despite being further from the Sun than Uranus, it's slightly warmer due to internal heat.
fun_facts_tr: Neptün, gözlem yerine matematiksel tahminle keşfedilen ilk gezegendir.
En büyük uydusu Triton ters yönde döner; muhtemelen yakalanmış bir Kuiper Kuşağı nesnesidir.
Güneş'e Uranüs'ten daha uzak olmasına rağmen, iç ısısı sayesinde biraz daha sıcaktır.

missions.name: Voyager 2
missions.result: The only spacecraft to visit Neptune - discovered the Great Dark Spot and active geysers on Triton.

sound_signature.why: 165-year orbit gives the longest-period notes in the entire system - produces deep, slow, sustained pieces with rich harmonic layers.

hero.caption: Neptune from Voyager 2 - the Great Dark Spot was the size of Earth.

gallery.caption: Triton, Neptune's largest moon - geyser-active and rotating opposite to the planet.
Neptune by JWST - the rings are once again clearly visible after Voyager 2.
